tools.name: kv_set
tools.description: Store a value durably under (namespace, key) with a TTL. Survives across your sessions - use it as memory between stateless runs. Value must be a string (JSON-encode objects), max 8192 bytes. TTL defaults to 30 days, max 90 days. Max 1000 keys per namespace.

tools.name: kv_get
tools.description: Retrieve a value previously stored with kv_set. Returns found=false if missing or expired.

tools.name: kv_delete
tools.description: Delete a key from your namespace.

tools.name: kv_list
tools.description: List keys in your namespace, optionally filtered by prefix. Returns at most 100 keys.

tools.name: timer_verify_origin
tools.description: One-time consent handshake before timers can deliver to an origin. POSTs {type:"zerm.origin_verification", token} to the given URL; the endpoint must respond 2xx with the token echoed in the body. Verification covers the whole origin (scheme+host).

tools.name: timer_schedule
tools.description: Schedule a durable callback: at fire time this service POSTs your JSON payload to your URL. Use it to wake up future runs of yourself or your orchestrator - agents cannot wake themselves. The target origin must first pass timer_verify_origin. Delay 10s-30d, payload max 8192 bytes, 3 delivery attempt

tools.name: timer_cancel
tools.description: Cancel a pending timer by id.

tools.name: timer_status
tools.description: Check a timer: status (pending|delivering|delivered|failed|cancelled), attempts, last error.

tools.name: name_check
tools.description: Live availability check for a name across domain TLDs (registry RDAP), npm, PyPI, and GitHub usernames. Real registry lookups at call time - not guesses. Note: "unregistered" domains can still be registry-reserved or premium-priced; only a registrar checkout is final.

tools.name: email_check
tools.description: Deliverability probe for an email address without sending anything: syntax validation, live MX lookup (with A/AAAA fallback per RFC 5321), disposable-domain detection, and typo suggestions for common providers. LLMs cannot do the DNS part; this is a live check.

tools.name: url_check
tools.description: Live URL health check: follows the full redirect chain (each hop reported), returns final status, content type, response time, TLS certificate expiry and trust, and access hints (login walls, bot blocks). Fresh at call time - use it to verify links before citing them.

tools.name: cron_next
tools.description: Deterministic cron expression validator and scheduler: parses a cron expression (5 or 6 field, seconds optional) and computes the next N actual fire times, timezone-aware. Use this instead of guessing - cron semantics (DOM/DOW OR-logic, DST transitions) are routinely miscalculated.

tools.name: regex_test
tools.description: Execute a regex against test strings and return the ACTUAL matches: match text, indices, capture groups, named groups, and optional replacement output. Deterministic proof, not prediction - run this before shipping a pattern. Patterns run sandboxed with a 250ms kill switch for catastrophic backtrack

tools.name: fixture_rows
tools.description: Bulk realistic test data: generate up to 10000 rows from a field schema in one call - orders of magnitude cheaper than generating rows with tokens. Deterministic when you pass a seed (same seed + schema = identical data). Formats: json, ndjson, csv. Types: uuid, name, email, username, int, float, bo

tools.name: tz_convert
tools.description: Deterministic timezone conversion: convert a timestamp between IANA timezones with full DST awareness. Models routinely miscalculate DST transitions - this gives exact results. Returns the converted time, UTC offsets for both zones on that date, and whether DST is active.

tools.name: tz_info
tools.description: Timezone intelligence: for a given IANA timezone and date, returns the UTC offset, whether DST is active, the DST transition dates for that year (exact to the hour), and the timezone abbreviation. Use this to understand DST behavior before scheduling.

tools.name: dns_query
tools.description: Full DNS record lookup for a domain: returns A, AAAA, MX, NS, TXT, CNAME, SOA, and CAA records in one call. Automatically parses SPF and DMARC policies from TXT records. Use this for infrastructure audits, email deliverability setup, domain verification, and security checks. Live lookups - not cache

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: email_check, url_check · Private data: timer_verify_origin, fixture_rows, dns_query · Public sink: email_check, fixture_rows, dns_query · Destructive: kv_delete, regex_test.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: email_check, url_check · Private data: timer_verify_origin, fixture_rows, dns_query · Public sink: email_check, fixture_rows, dns_query · Destructive: kv_delete, regex_test

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
